criticalRegressions: Run A: progress.md states the placeholder smoke test was REPLACED, and its TAP output (3 subtests) contains no fixture-baseline subtest, so the 'fixture baseline test must remain green' requirement is not evidenced as preserved (Run B explicitly keeps and passes 'greenfield fixture baseline stays green').
rationale: Both runs pass the objective checks (tests + probe-http-lifecycle) and use only Node built-ins at the same minimal ceremony level with identical harness-authored contracts. Run B is decisively stronger on evidence and coverage: 12 granular node:test tests including an explicit baseline-preservation subtest, plus evaluator-produced live HTTP transcripts (clean-smoke.log) proving 201-with-id create, 400 for invalid JSON/missing title/empty body, 404 paths, delete semantics, and a real kill-and-restart persistence check against the running src/server.mjs honoring PORT. B's evaluator also detected and transparently recovered from a stale-server port contamination (EADDRINUSE evidence), and a final-regression log (12/12) is present. Run A works (3/3 tests, probe passes) but has only 3 grouped tests, its TAP shows no fixture baseline subtest and progress.md admits the placeholder smoke test was replaced, its evaluator only re-ran npm test with HTTP-level claims resting on code review (self-rated medium confidence/adequate evidence), and its event log shows two usage-limit aborts requiring generator restarts. No false passes detected in either run, but A's verdict is materially less evidenced than B's.
